lede: A look at September 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ferdinand Marcos and Eric Abidal, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: September 11 attacks
events.desc: The September 11 attacks, a series of coordinated terrorist attacks killing 2,977 people using four aircraft hijacked by 19 members of al-Qaeda. Two aircraft crash into the World Trade Center in New York City, a third crashes into The Pentagon in Arlington County, Virginia, and a fourth into a field near Shanksville, Pennsylvania.

events.title: Hurricane Iniki
events.desc: Hurricane Iniki strikes the island of Kauaʻi, Hawaii, killing 7 and causing $3.1bn in economic damages.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German troops occupy Corsica and Kosovo-Metohija ending the Italian occupation of Corsica.

events.title: Charles Lindbergh
events.desc: Charles Lindbergh makes his Des Moines speech accusing the British, Jews and FDR's administration of conspiring for war with Germany.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Australia invades German New Guinea, defeating a German contingent at the Battle of Bita Paka.

events.title: Switzerland
events.desc: In the Swiss state of Glarus, a rockslide buries parts of the village of Elm, destroying 83 buildings and killing 115 people.

events.title: Christiana Riot
events.desc: Christiana Resistance: Escaped slaves led by William Parker fight off and kill a slave owner who, with a federal marshal and an armed party, sought to seize three of his former slaves in Christiana, Pennsylvania, thereby creating a cause célèbre between slavery proponents and abolitionists.

events.title: Alexander Hamilton
events.desc: Alexander Hamilton is appointed the first United States Secretary of the Treasury.

events.title: Staten Island Peace Conference
events.desc: British–American peace conference on Staten Island fails to stop nascent American Revolutionary War.

events.title: Santiago
events.desc: Santiago, Chile, is attacked by indigenous warriors, led by Michimalonco, to free eight indigenous chiefs held captive by the Spaniards.

births.name: Ferdinand Marcos
births.line: Filipino soldier, lawyer, and politician, 10th President of the Philippines

births.name: Vinoba Bhave
births.line: Indian philosopher and Gandhian, Bharat Ratna Awardee

births.name: Queen Paola of Belgium
births.line: Queen of the Belgians from 1993 to 2013

deaths.text: Joe Zawinul, Austrian keyboard player and songwriter
1978 smallpox outbreak in the United Kingdom, English photographer
Salvador Allende, Chilean physician and politician, 29th President of Chile

observances.text: Christian feast days: Felix, Regula, and Exuperantius
Christian feast days: John Gabriel Perboyre (one of Martyr Saints of China)
Christian feast days: Our Lady of Coromoto
